title: Executive Order 11336—Delegating to the Secretary of Agriculture Certain Authority Relating to Emergency Livestock Feed

flags.justification: A narrow, criteria-based delegation triggered only by specific catastrophic conditions (drought, flood, hurricane, blizzard) affecting Indian grazing land.
Sec. 2 provides a real termination mechanism: assistance ends upon notice by the Secretary of Agriculture.
An emergency-assistance determination mechanism, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
Sec. 4 sets a specific end date (January 1, 1968) for the existing declaration, or an earlier date if fixed by notice.
Sec. 3 requires consultation with the Secretary of the Interior, a real cross-agency check.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A narrow, specific, bounded emergency-assistance delegation.
The required consultation with Interior provides well-coordinated, non-conflicting administration.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
